Name: Weedle
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: If this Pokémon has full HP, this attack does 40 more damage, and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

Name: Exeggcute
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.

Name: Treecko
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

Name: Grovyle
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Sceptile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
Flavor-Text: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

Name: Cacnea
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: By storing water in its body, this desert dweller can survive for 30 days without water.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 60 more damage and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day’s desert heat.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With cells similar to those of plants, it performs photosynthesis inside its body and creates pure air.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees F.

Name: Heatran-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its flame is usually out, but it starts shining when it absorbs life force from people or Pokémon.

Name: Lampent
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
If Zekrom is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

Name: Horsea
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Seadra
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional-medicine ingredients.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can’t be seen when it melts away into water.

Name: Wooper
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When walking on land, it covers its body with a poisonous film that keeps its skin from dehydrating.

Name: Quagsire
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [F] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn’t care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

Name: Glaceon
Ability-Effect: The Retreat Cost of each of your Team Plasma Pokémon in play is [C][C] less.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

Name: Tympole
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans and warns others of danger.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to immobilize its opponents.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: Does 30 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By putting power into its bumps, it creates vibrations and increases the power of its punches.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: Search your deck for a [W] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148° F.

Name: Kyurem
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

Name: Voltorb
Flavor-Text: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on contact.

Name: Electrode
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, that Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: By storing electricity in its body, it can shoot its bristlelike fur like a barrage of missiles.

Name: Chinchou
Flavor-Text: It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Thundurus-EX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Team Plasma Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
If Reshiram is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with lightning. It assists those who want to build an ideal world.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: While it does not prefer to fight, even one drop of the poison it secretes from its barbs can be fatal.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than Nidorino’s.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is armored with hard scales. It will protect the young in its burrow with its life.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It scans its surroundings by raising its ears out of the grass. Its toxic horn is for protection.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It has a violent disposition and stabs foes with its horn, which oozes poison upon impact.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

Name: Muk
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s so stinky! Muk’s body contains toxic elements, and any plant will wilt when it passes by.

Name: Mr. Mime
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

Name: Espeon
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its fur is so sensitive, it can feel minute shifts in the air and predict the weather…and its foes’ thoughts.

Name: Sableye
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: Its cells are all magnetic, and it communicates with others by using magnetic pulses.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its two brains are linked, amplifying its psychic power.

Name: Metagross
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Team Plasma card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You may not use an Ability with the same name during your turn.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: With four linked brains, it’s more intelligent than a supercomputer, and it uses calculations to analyze foes.

Name: Deoxys-EX
Ability-Effect: Your Team Plasma Pokémon’s attacks (excluding Deoxys-EX) do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yamask
Effects: Move 1 damage counter from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life.

Name: Yamask
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life.

Name: Cofagrigus
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack) you may Knock Out this Pokémon. If you do, put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: Whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her next turn, treat it as tails.
Flavor-Text: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: Does 40 more damage for each Nidoqueen on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

Name: Mankey
Flavor-Text: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

Name: Onix
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Opening its large mouth, it ingests massive amounts of soil and creates long tunnels.

Name: Makuhita
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It loves to match power with big-bodied Pokémon. It can knock a truck flying with its arm thrusts.

Name: Umbreon
Ability-Effect: Each of your Team Plasma Pokémon in play gets +20 HP.
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it’s filled with a mysterious power.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A smart and sneaky Pokémon, it makes its opponents flinch by suddenly showing the claws hidden in its paws.

Name: Weavile
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard as many Pokémon as you like from your hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Pokémon you discarded.
Flavor-Text: They communicate by clawing signs in boulders and work together to surround enemies.

Name: Absol
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: It appears from deep in the mountains to warn people about upcoming disasters it has sensed with its horn.

Name: Sandile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They live hidden under hot desert sands in order to keep their body temperature from dropping.

Name: Krokorok
Flavor-Text: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: Move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very violent Pokémon, they try to clamp down on anything that moves in front of their eyes.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a [N] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

Name: Deino
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Lacking sight, it’s unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Lacking sight, it’s unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

Name: Hydreigon
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.
Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path!

Name: Steelix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: The iron it ingested with the soil it swallowed transformed its body and made it harder than diamonds.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

Name: Dratini
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is called the “Mirage Pokémon” because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If its body takes on an aura, the weather changes instantly. It is said to live in seas and lakes.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

Name: Kingdra
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of [W] Energy cards and [L] Energy cards in your discard pile. Then, shuffle all of those cards back into your deck.
This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives in caves on the seafloor and creates giant whirlpools every time it moves.

Name: Latias-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon with Abilities.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Latios-EX
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rattata
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It gnaws on hard objects to wear down its fangs, which grow constantly during its lifetime.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Put a Team Plasma Pokémon, a Team Plasma Trainer card, and a Team Plasma Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: With its long fangs, this surprisingly violent Pokémon can gnaw away even thick concrete with ease.

Name: Eevee
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for 3 Pokémon of different types that evolve from Eevee. Reveal them and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: Each player draws 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It marks time precisely. Some countries consider it to be a wise friend, versed in the world’s ways.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

Name: Miltank
Effects: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that kids who drink Miltank’s milk grow up to become hearty, healthy adults.

Name: Kecleon
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, this Pokémon is the same type as the your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. If this Pokémon has the necessary Energy to use that attack, use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: It can freely change its body’s color. The zigzag pattern on its belly doesn’t change, however.

Name: Starly
Flavor-Text: Because they are weak individually, they form groups. However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

Name: Staravia
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: Your opponent shuffles the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: It never stops attacking even if it is injured. It fusses over the shape of its comb.

Name: Tornadus-EX
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Float Stone
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Frozen City
Description: Whenever any player attaches an Energy from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon (excluding Team Plasma Pokémon) put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Ghetsis
Description: Your opponent reveals his or her hand and shuffles all Item cards found there into his or her deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Item cards your opponent shuffled into his or her deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Shadow Triad
Description: Put a Team Plasma card from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Superior Energy Retrieval
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can’t choose a card you discarded with the effect of this card.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Team Plasma Badge
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to is a Team Plasma Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Team Plasma Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Team Plasma Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Life Dew
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Rock Guard
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Heatran-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Thundurus-EX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Team Plasma Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Deoxys-EX
Ability-Effect: Your Team Plasma Pokémon’s attacks (excluding Deoxys-EX) do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Latias-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon with Abilities.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Latios-EX
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tornadus-EX
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ghetsis
Description: Your opponent reveals his or her hand and shuffles all Item cards found there into his or her deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Item cards your opponent shuffled into his or her deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Juniper
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Empoleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. Then, draw 2 cards.
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Sigilyph
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Garbodor
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Max Potion
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
